text: - iv. Scope of Coverage and Limits of Insurance. The coverage provided to the Additional Insureds must be at least as broad as that provided to the first named insured on each policy.  If any policy provided in compliance with this Section states that the coverage provided to an additional insured shall be no broader than that required by contract, or words of similar meaning, the parties agree that nothing in this Section is intended to restrict or limit the breadth of such coverage.  The limits of insurance stated above for each type of insurance are minimum limits only.  If a policy provides greater limits, then the Additional Insureds shall be entitled to, or to share in, the full limits of such policy, and this Section shall be deemed to require such full limits.
